column1: # aav_variant_guides.tsv — candidate cardiomyopathy variants for downstream
# ribozyme guide design under LVAD scenario AAV_BTR_GENE_THERAPY.
# HONEST SCOPE (g8 / f2 / paradigm-shift gap):
#   No LVAD-cohort gene therapy with explant endpoint has ever been approved
#   or demonstrated in humans (AAV_BTR_GENE_THERAPY.tape §5 novelty rationale).
#   This catalogue is an IN-SILICO target shortlist for ribozyme-guide MFE +
#   off-target screening; entries here are NOT therapeutic candidates, NOT
#   approved targets, and NOT validated in any LVAD-recovery setting.
# CITATION POLICY (g3):
#   Each row cites a primary peer-reviewed source establishing the gene's
#   pathogenic role in cardiomyopathy. Specific HGVS variants are taken from
#   the cited paper when explicitly named there; broader variant classes
#   (e.g. "TTN truncating, A-band exons") are cited to the seminal series
#   paper. Where the specific variant cannot be tied to a single primary
#   source it is omitted rather than fabricated.
# Columns:
#   gene          HGNC symbol
#   variant       short variant label (HGVS-style or class)
#   hgvs_c        HGVS coding-level notation (or "class:<descriptor>")
#   hgvs_p        HGVS protein-level notation
#   inheritance   AD = autosomal dominant; AR = autosomal recessive
#   rationale     why this is a candidate for AAV-BTR guide design
#   citation      primary peer-reviewed source (author + journal + year)
gene
TTN
column6: TTNtv in A-band exons are the single most common Mendelian cause of DCM (~25% of familial DCM); LVAD recipients are enriched for TTNtv carriers — high-yield class for trans-splicing ribozyme rescue exploration.
column7: Herman DS et al. 2012 NEJM 366(7):619-628 doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1110186

column1: MYH7
column6: Founding HCM missense mutation; can transition to burnt-out / dilated phenotype requiring LVAD; canonical target for allele-specific suppression strategies.
column7: Geisterfer-Lowrance AA et al. 1990 Cell 62(5):999-1006 doi:10.1016/0092-8674(90)90274-i

column1: MYBPC3
column6: Dutch founder frameshift accounting for ~25% of Dutch HCM; haploinsufficiency mechanism; large carrier pool with documented LVAD subset.

column1: LMNA
column6: LMNA-DCM with conduction-system disease is high-mortality, early-LVAD subgroup; trans-splicing or allele-specific suppression candidates; class-level entry — specific variant requires per-patient sequencing.

column1: DSP
column6: Desmoplakin truncating variants define a distinct fibrotic/inflammatory ALVC subtype with high LV involvement and end-stage progression; haploinsufficiency target.
column7: Smith ED et al. 2020 Circulation 141(23):1872-1884 doi:10.1161/CIRCULATIONAHA.119.044934
